rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. TODD BRABEC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Associations Further Amended Discovery Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. Two, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 15, 2025, Line 10. PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON's Motion To Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Associations Further Amended Discovery Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. Two, And Request For Sanctions. Continued to June 10, 2025, on the court's motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW CARO VS. IMPERIAL PARKING (U.S), LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Pursuant To Crc 3.770
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 15, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ANDREW CARO's Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Pursuant To Crc 3.770. Plaintiff Andrew Caro's unopposed Motion Entire Action Pursuant To CRC 3.770 is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court orders the case dismissed as follows: (1) Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims are dismissed without prejudice; (3) Plaintiff's individual PAGA claims are dismissed without prejudice; (3) Plaintiff's class claims are dismissed without prejudice; (4) Plaintiff's individual (non) PAGA claims are dismissed without prejudice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL PATRICK MORIARTY ET AL VS. DAMION DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 15, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT DAMION DAVIS' MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Defense counsel Jennnifer Sommer and Smith, Koyama & Costello's motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Defendant Damion Davis is DENIED. However, all counsel are ORDERED TO APPEAR. Counsel made a general appearance for Defendant Damion Davis. Counsel now seeks to be relieved as his attorney because, it turns out, Davis is not "CSAA insured," he cannot be indemnified by CSAA, and he "cannot be defended under the CSAA policy." Counsel contends Plaintiff served the wrong "Damion Davis," and that's how all this started. Counsel moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.610. Section 2034.610 is a discovery statute covering augmentation or amendments to expert witness lists or declarations. Moving counsel here has provided no legal basis for an order relieving them as counsel. Clearly, section 2034.610 has not application. In any event, Moving Counsel has not provided a basis for an order authorizing their withdrawal. Their failure to exercise due diligence before making a general appearance is not a basis for a judicial order authorizing withdrawal. That said, all counsel of record are ordered to meet and confer immediately to resolve the matter whether the person for whom Moving Counsel made a general appearance, Damion Davis, is a proper party and, if not, how to resolve the issue of his status as a party. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Moving counsel is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Terminating Sanctions To Wish Us Holdings Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 15, 2025, Line 5. 1 - PLAINTIFF GENOA MARTELL's Motion For Terminating Sanctions To Wish Us Holdings LLLC. Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions and entry of default against defendant Wish US Holdings LLC is DENIED. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that she satisfied the meet and confer obligation, that is the obligation to make reasonable and good efforts to informally resolve the discovery dispute before presenting it to the court. On April 8, 2025, plaintiff sent a purported meet and confer letter with attachments to defendant that identified numerous alleged deficiencies in defendant's discovery responses. (Martell Decl., Ex. 3.) The letter arbitrarily imposed a deadline of April 14, 2025, to cure each deficiency. In response, by e-mail dated April 10, 2025, defense counsel noted the unfairness of requiring amended responses in less than a week, stated that he would attempt to respond to the issues by April 17, 2025, and extended the deadline for plaintiff to bring a motion to May 19, 2025. (Martell Decl., Ex. 3A.) In fact, on April 17, 2025, defendant did respond to plaintiff and agreed to provide some amended responses. (Wahl Decl., Exs. A-D.) On April 29, 2025, defendant served supplemental responses. (Wahl Decl., par. 7.) Rather than engage in the discovery process in good faith, plaintiff rushed to court on April 15, 2025, and filed the instant motion-precisely the conduct the legislature prohibited when it imposed the meet-and-confer duty. Plaintiff's attempt to employ the "nuclear option" while defendant was attempting to respond to plaintiff's concerns is the antithesis of meeting and conferring in good faith. The motion is therefore denied. (CCP 2016.040 ["A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion."]; Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1294 ["[T]he statute requires that there be a serious effort at negotiation and informal resolution."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE FULLER VS. BAMBOO INSURANCE, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: For Leave To File A Supplemental Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 15, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF MICHELLE FULLER's For Leave To File A Supplemental Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a supplemental complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff's motion amounts to a motion for reconsideration and she failed to make the showing necessary for a motion for reconsideration. On April 11, 2025, the court heard and denied plaintiff's motion for leave to file a supplemental complaint. The court "denied" the prior motion; it was not denied "without prejudice." The instant renewed motion for the same relief does not comply with CCP 1008(b) in that it does not "show by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (See Fuller Decl.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. MAX ROTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Civil Proceeding
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, May 15, 2025, Line 8 DEFENDANT MAX ROTH's Motion For Stay Of Civil Proceeding. Defendant Max V. Roth's motion for stay is GRANTED. In Avant! Corp v. Sup. Crt, (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 876, 887, the court listed the following factors when ruling for consideration when adjudicating a request for a stay in a context akin to the circumstances of the instant case: (1) defendant's Fifth Amendment interest; (2) the interest of plaintiff in proceeding expeditiously and conducting discovery and the potential prejudice to plaintiffs of a delay; (3) the convenience of the court in the management of its cases, and the efficient use of judicial resources; (4) the interests of persons not parties to the civil litigation; and (5) the interest of the public in the pending civil and criminal litigation. Upon considering these factors, the court stays prosecution of this action until further order of the court. The court continues this motion to January 15, 2026, to determine the status of the criminal action and whether a continued stay is warranted. By January 8, 2026, each side may file a supplemental brief up to 7 pages regarding the status of the criminal action or any other matter germane to continuance of the stay. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
